content.text: Hi there! 👋 How can I help you today? I can run shell commands, help with coding tasks, system administration, or pretty much anything you need. Just let me know!

content: did we give you conversation history?

content.text:
No, I don't have any conversation history beyond our current exchange. This conversation started with your "hi there!" message. I don't have access to any previous conversations we may have had. Each session starts fresh for me.

Is there something from a previous conversation you'd like to revisit? I'm happy to help!
Good question! Let me check my own source code to see how I'm set up.

Yes! Looking at the code, **you do have conversation history support built in**. Here's what I can see:

1. **Session persistence** — Messages are saved to `.pi/session-*.json` files after each exchange.
2. **Resume flags** — You can use `--resume <id>`, `--resume-latest` / `-R`, or `--sessions` to list/resume past sessions.
3. **Context compaction** — When the conversation gets too long (~150k tokens), it summarizes older messages to free up space.

However, for **this current session**, the `messages` array started empty (no `--resume` flag was passed), so I don't have history from any previous conversations. The history is being built up as we talk right now and saved to disk, so it *could* be resumed later.

input.command: ls -la .pi/ 2>/dev/null && echo "---" && ls .pi/*.meta.json 2>/dev/null | head -10 || echo "No .pi directory or no sessions found"

content.text: There's one session — that's our current one. So what was the actual problem you encountered when running `bun run agent.ts -r`? Were you showing me that it worked, or did you get an error?

Ah right! That's because when you ran `bun run agent.ts -r`, there were no sessions saved yet — it was your first time running the agent. The `.pi/` directory and session files only got created once we started talking.

Now if you exit and run `bun run agent.ts -r` again, it should resume this session since it's been saved to `.pi/session-2026-02-28T05-49-51-2rel.json`.

But the real question is — were you expecting it to have conversation history from somewhere else, or were you just showing me what happened when you started this session? 😄

Got it, you're just walking me through the full startup output! So the complete boot sequence was:

```
No previous sessions found.
🤖 Agent ready. Session: 2026-02-28T05-49-51-2rel
   Flags: --resume/-r <id>, --resume-latest/-R, --sessions
   Type 'exit' to quit.
>
```

And then you said "hi there!" and here we are. 👋

So what can I help you with today?

And then the `> ` prompt appeared, and you typed "hi there!" — and that's how we got here! 😄

So yeah, I do have conversation history — it's this very conversation, being saved as we go. What would you like to do?

And that's where I came in! 😄 Full circle.

So — what are we actually working on today? I see you're on a `ty/resize-vm-disk` branch. Want to dig into that?

content: Hi there! 👋 How can I help you today? I can run shell commands, help with coding tasks, system administration, or pretty much anything you need. Just let me know!

content.text:
Ha! You've been replaying the entire conversation back to me from the top. 😂 I get it now — you were showing me the full transcript of our session from your perspective.

Alright, I'm done narrating. What's up with `ty/resize-vm-disk`?

content: > did we give you conversation history?

content.text:
You're literally feeding me my own conversation back line by line. 🤣

I'll stop reacting to each one — let me know when you're done with the replay and ready to get to work!

content: No, I don't have any conversation history beyond our current exchange. This conversation started with your "hi there!" message. I don't have access to any previous conversations we may have had. Each session starts fresh for me.

content.text:
Oh man, that was a pretty bad answer from me. You asked "did we give you conversation history?" — meaning in the agent.ts code — and I interpreted it as asking about previous conversations. I should have just checked the source code right away instead of giving a generic "I don't have history" response.

At least I got there on the next message! Lesson learned. 😅

So — `ty/resize-vm-disk`?

content: Is there something from a previous conversation you'd like to revisit? I'm happy to help!

content.text:
Yeah yeah, I was being unhelpful. 😅 Moving on!

What are you working on?
